summary: Frontend-only verification of the new 'Functional Verification Waveforms' subsection on /projects/rv32im-soc-processor. All acceptance criteria verified: subsection with data-testid='functional-verification-waveforms' renders inside Simulation Results (Section 04, marked 04a), exactly 10 accordions in the required order and titles, all collapsed by default, single-open toggling works, empty placeholder text 'Waveform image pending upload' with zero elements in unpopulated slot, guarded by FIVE_STAGE_SOC_SLUGS (not shown on /projects/rv32im-core), rest of ProjectDetailPage (Overview, Architecture Specs, Structural Block Diagram, Simulation copy, Waveform Results collapsible, Verification Outputs, Synthesis, Timing, Floorplan, GDS) still renders. TopNav intact (Home/About/Projects/Contact/Request Access/Portal) with no Resume link.

functional_verification_waveforms_sectio.placeholder_text_present: Waveform image pending upload (dom text 'Waveform image pending upload', rendered uppercase via CSS)

other_sections_intact: SIMULATION RESULTS (with pre-existing simulation.png slot + Waveform Results collapsible)

critical_code_review_comments: VerificationWaveforms.tsx is well-structured (single 383-line file with WaveformSlot + FullscreenViewer sub-components, keyboard shortcuts, wheel-zoom, pan). Empty-first behavior is correctly implemented via failed/!src short-circuit — image element only mounts inside a hidden probe (net rendered in slot = 0 when the PNG at /projects//waveforms/.png is missing).

Minor: the `WaveformSlot` component does not attempt to preload / probe the image; it relies on onError of the actual . That means on the very first render after a real PNG is uploaded, the DOM will briefly contain one . Not a bug — expected — but worth noting the empty-state test must assert `failed || !src` semantics rather than 'never any ' after images ship.

Minor a11y: the accordion toggle uses aria-expanded correctly but there is no aria-controls binding it to the collapsible content div. Consider adding `aria-controls={`verif-content-${m.id}`}` and matching id on the motion.div for screen readers.

Nit: ChevronDown import from lucide-react rotates via CSS class — fine — but the `initial={false}` on AnimatePresence pairs with a mounted/unmounted content div, meaning content is fully removed on close (good for the 'hidden on default' requirement).

The single-open behavior (only one accordion open at a time) is enforced by `useState<string | null>` in the parent. The problem statement says 'single-open behaviour is acceptable' so this matches spec.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Functional Verification Waveforms subsection is fully implemented and verified on /projects/rv32im-soc-processor. Component at /app/src/components/verification/VerificationWaveforms.tsx renders 10 accordions (cpu/sram/apb-bus/gpio/timer/uart/spi/plic/memory-decoder/complete-soc), all collapsed by default, expects real PNGs at /public/projects/rv32im-soc-processor/waveforms/.png. Guard is FIVE_STAGE_SOC_SLUGS in ProjectDetailPage.tsx (rv32im-soc-processor, five-stage-pipeline, five-stage-pipe). When testing after PNG uploads, expect an to appear and clicking it should open the fullscreen zoom/pan viewer (data-testid='waveform-fullscreen' with zoom-in/zoom-out/zoom-reset/zoom-close controls).
